title: Dactylorhiza × mortonii
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × mortonii is a hybrid orchid resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza maculata. It is a terrestrial orchid that grows in wet, acidic soils. The classification of Dactylorhiza × mortonii can be broken down into its botanical classification, which includes the kingdom Plantae, class Liliopsida, order Asparagales, family Orchidaceae, and genus Dactylorhiza. At the species level, the hybrid is denoted by its name 'Dactylorhiza × mortonii', indicating it is a hybrid between two different species of Dactylorhiza. This hybrid orchid is native to the United Kingdom and Ireland.

intro: Dactylorhiza × mortonii, also known as Morton's Spotted Orchid, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza maculata. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × mortonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried powder, and is often used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations.
